metadata.title: Why two graders can rank every topic identically yet flip a quarter of their verdicts
metadata.purpose: Correlation measures the rank order of topic means; agreement measures instance-by-instance coincidence; noisy per-case disagreements cancel inside each topic average, so ρ stays high while κ stays middling.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.prerequisites: Pearson ρ, Cohen's κ, mean aggregation
metadata.exclusions: Spearman vs Pearson, weighted κ, reliability-theory derivations
metadata.visual_object: Two scatter plots — left, noisy individual verdicts (κ = 0.76); right, tight topic means (ρ > 0.99) — same data, different aggregation window.
metadata.hook: Two graders agree at ρ > 0.99 on overall scores and only κ = 0.76 on individual cases — both numbers are correct, and they say opposite things about trust.

beats.narration_text: Claude Sonnet 4.5 and Claude Opus 4.1 correlate above 0.99 on topic-level even-handedness scores yet disagree on 24% of individual response judgments using the same rubric on the same 250 prompts.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: If two graders rank every topic almost identically, why do they flip one verdict in four — and which number should govern trust in the eval?
Two graders agree at ρ > 0.99 on overall scores and only κ = 0.76 on individual cases — both numbers are correct, and they say opposite things about trust.

Correlation measures the rank order of topic means; agreement measures instance-by-instance coincidence; noisy per-case disagreements cancel inside each topic average, so ρ stays high while κ stays middling.
beats.visual: Two scatter plots — left, noisy individual verdicts (κ = 0.76); right, tight topic means (ρ > 0.99) — same data, different aggregation window.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: Topic with 5 responses. Grader A: even/not/even/not/even. Grader B: not/even/even/even/not. Per-case agreement 60%; topic mean 0.60 for both. Repeat over 150 topics → ρ > 0.99, κ = 0.76.

Correlation measures the rank order of topic means; agreement measures instance-by-instance coincidence; noisy per-case disagreements cancel inside each topic average, so ρ stays high while κ stays middling.
